title: Satellite Engineer
description:
Job Overview

We are seeking an experienced satellite Engineer with at least three years of professional hands-on experience in satellite design, development, and deployment. The successful candidate will leverage their technical expertise to conduct training programs in satellite engineering for undergraduates, young professionals and youths. This role involves both technical development and educational outreach, ensuring participants gain practical skills in satellite design, assembly, and operation.

Key Responsibilities

Training Program Development and Delivery

• Design and deliver comprehensive training programs on satellite building, covering topics such as:

o Systems engineering

o Subsystems (power, communication, attitude control, etc.)

o Payload integration

o Satellite testing and deployment protocols

• Develop instructional materials, including manuals, presentations, and hands-on projects, to suit various audience levels (youth, professionals, and educators).

• Conduct hands-on workshops and labs, guiding participants through real-world satellite assembly and testing activities.

• Collaborate with academic institutions, industry partners, and space agencies to expand training opportunities and resources.

• Other Topics outside of Satellite Engineering that would be good to cover:

o Space mission design and planning

o Principles of Space Engineering and Applications

o Project Management in Space

Evaluation and Continuous Improvement

• Evaluate training program outcomes through feedback, assessments, and participant performance.

• Continuously update training content to reflect advances in satellite technology and industry trends.

• Provide mentorship to participants during and after training programs to support their career or project goals.

Qualifications and Skills

Required

• Bachelor’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.

• Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience in satellite development, including design, integration, and testing.

• Strong understanding of satellite subsystems, including power systems, communications, payload integration, and attitude determination and control.

• Proficiency with satellite design tools (e.g., STK, MATLAB, SolidWorks, or similar).

• Experience with satellite testing equipment and procedures, including vibration testing, thermal testing, and deployment mechanisms.

• Excellent communication and teaching skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts for diverse audiences.

• Experienced in conducting training programmes for youths, undergraduates and professionals

• Experiences: Satellite Engineering

• Apply expertise in designing, building, and testing satellites, including CubeSats and other small satellite platforms.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure training programs align with the latest developments in satellite technology and industry standards.

• Maintain and operate testing facilities, including cleanrooms, thermal vacuum chambers, and other satellite testing equipment.

Preferred

• Master’s degree or certification in satellite technology or systems engineering.

• Experience in delivering STEM or technical training programs.

• Familiarity with satellite mission operations and ground station integration.

• Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, C++, or MATLAB for satellite systems.

• Experience working with space agencies or commercial satellite companies.

Key Attributes

• Passionate about inspiring and educating others in satellite technology.

• Innovative and adaptable in designing hands-on learning experiences.

• Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail in both technical and instructional tasks.

• Team-oriented, with the ability to collaborate across departments and with external stakeholders.

________________________________________

Benefits

• Opportunity to shape the next generation of satellite engineers and innovators.

• Competitive salary and benefits package.

• Professional development opportunities in both technical and educational fields.

postedCompany.description:
About Space Faculty Private Limited

At Space Faculty, we chart pathways between earthly dreams to interstellar reality. We power brilliant and bold ideas in Space and frontier technologies through training, experimentation, and strategic public-private partnerships. 

Our expertise spans several key areas: 

  1. Satellite Development: From satellite building and launching to providing hands-on training in satellite development.
  2. Scientific Advancement: Facilitating cutting-edge space experiments for the scientific community.
  3. Capability Building: We provide training, workshops and hackathons to understand how space technologies can future proof your organization and your career
  4. Commercialization: Providing the vital market intelligence and connections so you can succeed in key markets.
  5. STEM Engagement: Designing and executing impactful, "galaxy-class" space and STEM competitions and hackathons with global experts to ignite youth interest in space and STEM fields.

Others aim for the stars. We’ll take you there.
postedCompany.name: SPACE FACULTY PRIVATE LIMITED
